ddls:
CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "product_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of product */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Unique serial number for the product */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date the product was purchased */,
  "warranty_expiration_date" TEXT /* Date the warranty for the product expires */,
  "current_location_id" INTEGER /* Current location of the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_current_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("current_location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_product_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_type_id") REFERENCES product_types ("product_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_types" (
  "product_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product type */,
  "product_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the product type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product type */,
  "average_refurbishment_time" INTEGER /* Average time it takes to refurbish products of this type */,
  "refurbishment_cost" TEXT /* Average cost to refurbish products of this type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grading_templates" (
  "template_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grading template */,
  "template_name" TEXT /* Name of the grading template */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the grading template */,
  "product_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of product the template is designed for */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the template is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("template_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grading_criteria" (
  "criterion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grading criterion */,
  "template_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the grading template */,
  "criterion_name" TEXT /* Name of the grading criterion */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the grading criterion */,
  "weight" TEXT /* Weight of the criterion in the overall grading */,
  "is_mandatory" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the criterion is mandatory */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("criterion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_grading_criteria_template_id FOREIGN KEY ("template_id") REFERENCES grading_templates ("template_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grades" (
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grade */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product */,
  "template_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the grading template */,
  "grade_name" TEXT /* Name of the grade (e.g., Excellent, Fair) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the grade */,
  "grade_score" TEXT /* Numerical score for the grade */,
  "graded_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who assigned the grade */,
  "graded_on" TEXT /* Date the grade was assigned */,
  "next_inspection_date" TEXT /* Date the product is due for the next inspection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("grade_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_grades_template_id FOREIGN KEY ("template_id") REFERENCES grading_templates ("template_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_grades_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grade_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grade change event */,
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the grade */,
  "grade_date" TEXT /* Date the grade was changed */,
  "reason_for_change" TEXT /* Reason for the grade change */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who updated the grade */,
  "updated_on" TEXT /* Date the grade was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_grade_history_grade_id FOREIGN KEY ("grade_id") REFERENCES grades ("grade_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager responsible for the location */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number for the location */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address for the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_locations" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location */,
  "arrival_date" TEXT /* Date the product arrived at the location */,
  "departure_date" TEXT /* Date the product left the location */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the product at the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id", "location_id")
)
